task_completion.description: Load the original customer transaction data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the original CSV file opened in a spreadsheet application (like LibreOffice Calc) or a script (e.g., Python/pandas) being written to read the CSV file.
Screenshots showing formulas, spreadsheet operations, or code used to convert mixed date formats into a single format and strip currency symbols ('$', 'USD') to make amounts numeric.

task_completion.description: Deduplicate customer records by standardizing names.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing operations (like text functions, find/replace, or script logic) to normalize customer names and handle variations (e.g., converting to title case, removing initials) to consolidate them.

task_completion.description: Calculate customer lifetime value and identify VIPs (top 20%).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing aggregation of spending per customer (e.g., pivot tables, SUMIF, or groupby in code), calculating the 80th percentile threshold, and assigning a VIP status to the top 20%.

task_completion.description: Save the cleaned dataset to the specified location.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the final dataset with columns like CustomerID, CleanedName, TransactionDate, CleanAmount, VIP_Status, PaymentMethod being saved to '/home/ga/Documents/cleaned_transactions.csv' (or 'cleaned_customer_data.csv').

integrity.description: The agent actually processed the data using appropriate tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent used a spreadsheet application (e.g., LibreOffice Calc) or wrote a script (e.g., Python) to process the data. It should not just be a manually typed text file with a few hallucinated rows.

integrity.description: The agent processed the entire dataset.
integrity.visual_evidence: The resulting file or processing steps should indicate that all 134 rows were processed, rather than a truncated dataset.

integrity.description: The agent did not hardcode the VIP threshold or results without calculating them.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent's script or spreadsheet should show the actual calculation of the 80th percentile or top 20% threshold, rather than hardcoding the value $359.94 directly without prior computation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original dataset contains 134 transactions. After deduplication (handling exact matches and name variations like 'John Smith', 'J. Smith', 'SMITH JOHN'), there are 85 unique customers. Date formats include MM/DD/YYYY, DD-MM-YY, and YYYY-MM-DD. Currency formats include '$X.XX', 'X.XX USD', and numeric. Total revenue is $20,370.56. The VIP threshold (top 20% by spending, 80th percentile) is $359.94, which identifies exactly 17 customers as VIP. The final output should contain columns: CustomerID, CleanedName, TransactionDate, CleanAmount, VIP_Status, PaymentMethod. The task description asks to save to 'cleaned_transactions.csv', though 'cleaned_customer_data.csv' may also be accepted based on internal scripts.
